etymology.description: semantic 巾(巾) (jīn, "cloth") + phonetic 尚(尚) (shàng) -> originally "lower garment" -> borrowed for "long-lasting; frequently" -> "ordinary; usual"
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 巾(巾) (jīn, "cloth") conveys the original idea of a cloth-like lower garment, which was later phonetically borrowed with 尚(尚) (shàng) for its sound rather than meaning, expanding to "long-lasting" and thus "ordinary; usual".

examples.chinese: 他是一个非常普通的人(他是一個非常普通的人)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì yī gè fēicháng pǔtōng de rén.
examples.english: He is a very ordinary person.

examples.chinese: 我们常常去那家咖啡店(我們常常去那家咖啡店)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen chángcháng qù nà jiā kāfēidiàn.
examples.english: We usually go to that coffee shop.

examples.chinese: 这个问题在日常生活中很常见(這個問題在日常生活中很常見)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège wèntí zài rìcháng shēnghuó zhōng hěn chángjiàn.
examples.english: This issue is very common in everyday life.
